title: Introduction to Tailwind CSS
content:
### What is Tailwind CSS?

Tailwind CSS is a utility-first CSS framework that provides low-level, composable utility classes to build custom designs directly in your HTML. Unlike traditional CSS frameworks like Bootstrap that offer pre-built components, Tailwind gives you building blocks to create your own.

### Utility-First Approach

Instead of writing custom CSS, you apply pre-existing utility classes directly in your markup. Each utility class maps to a single CSS property-value pair:

```html
<button class="bg-blue-500 text-white font-bold py-2 px-4 rounded">
  Click Me
</button>
```

### Atomic CSS

Tailwind follows atomic CSS principles where each class does one thing. `.bg-blue-500` sets the background color, `.text-white` sets text color, `.font-bold` sets font weight, etc.

### Benefits Over Traditional CSS

- **No naming conventions** - No more BEM or CSS methodology debates
- **Smaller bundles** - JIT engine purges unused styles
- **Consistent design system** - Built-in spacing, color, and typography scales
- **Rapid prototyping** - Style directly in markup without switching files
- **Responsive by default** - Prefixes for every breakpoint on any utility

### Comparison with Bootstrap

| Feature | Tailwind | Bootstrap |
|---------|----------|-----------|
| Approach | Utility-first | Component-first |
| Customization | Highly configurable | Override variables |
| Output size | Tiny (purged) | Larger (includes unused) |
| Learning curve | Steeper (many utilities) | Easier (pre-built components) |
| Design uniqueness | Easy to achieve | Harder to differentiate |

### Design System Philosophy

Tailwind enforces a consistent design system through its configuration file. You define your colors, spacing, typography, and breakpoints in one place, ensuring consistency across your entire project.

### JIT (Just-In-Time) Engine

Tailwind v3 introduced a JIT compiler that generates styles on-demand as you author your HTML. This means:
- **Fast build times** - No full purging step needed
- **All variants work** - Every combination is generated when used
- **Arbitrary values** - Use any value with square bracket syntax: `w-[500px]`
- **Smaller CSS** - Only the classes you use are included
codeExample:
Tailwind CSS

A utility-first CSS framework for rapid UI development.

quiz.question: What approach does Tailwind CSS use for styling?
What does the JIT engine in Tailwind CSS v3 do?
options: Generates styles on-demand as you author HTML
